Payment {$510.00} made XXXX Payment {$10000.00} XXXX - verified with customer service representative. Requested lowered APR and was denied. Requested account be closed. Customer support verified payment, account balance {$0.00} and paid in full. Account closed. 

Statement received XXXX - balance {$140.00}. Contacted Citi Bank again, and was told that these were residual interest charges. Requested a waiver due to the lack of transparent communication around residual interest. This was denied. 

understand that residual interest is a legally permissible practice, but I believe it is deceptive and predatory in nature, especially when : The issuer does not clearly disclose how or when residual interest is calculated ; The consumer takes every step to pay off the balance in good faith ; The remaining interest is minimal and requested to be waived as a one-time courtesy. 

This practice seems to take advantage of consumers good-faith efforts to pay off debt. It feels less like fair interest accrual and more like an engineered trap that is not adequately explained during the payment process.

I respectfully ask the CFPB to review the policies of Citi Strata regarding residual interest and investigate whether their practices align with the spirit of fair, transparent consumer credit. I also request that you compel them to offer clearer disclosures and to allow for a reasonable, one-time waiver of residual interest, especially for customers acting in good faith.

This contradictory reporting violates my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and is damaging my creditworthiness unfairly. I previously disputed this with Self Financial through XXXX XXXX? Management and formally requested they either verify the late payment with documentation or delete it if they could not prove its accuracy. 

To date, I have received no documentation to validate this negative mark. 
FCRA Violations Involved : 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) Furnishers must report with maximum possible accuracy. Reporting different information across bureaus about the same account is inherently inaccurate and misleading.

15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) It is illegal for furnishers to report information they know to be inaccurate or can not verify.

15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) Upon notice of a dispute, the data furnisher is required to : Conduct a thorough investigation Review all relevant information Report results to the credit bureaus Correct or delete any unverifiable or inaccurate data 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) If information is disputed and can not be verified, it must be removed or corrected. 

Legal Support : In XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ), the court reaffirmed that consumers have a right to accuracy in their credit reports and that unverifiable data must be removed to protect consumers from financial harm.

I am reaching to you for assistance with an issue with US Bank that is non-compliant with XXXX statute and they ignored my repeated requests for XXXX benefits while I was on active duty service. Please see reference to the statute as follows : " 527. Maximum rate of interest on debts incurred before military service [ Sec. 207 ] ( a ) Interest rate limitation.

( 1 ) Limitation to 6 percent. An obligation or liability bearing interest at a rate in excess of 6 percent per year that is incurred by a servicemember, or the servicemember and the servicemember 's spouse jointly, before the servicemember enters military service shall not bear interest at a rate in excess of 6 percent ( A ) during the period of military service and one year thereafter, in the case of an obligation or liability consisting of a mortgage, trust deed, or other security in the nature of a mortgage; or ( B ) during the period of military service, in the case of any other obligation or liability.5 ( 2 ) Forgiveness of interest in excess of 6 percent. Interest at a rate in excess of 6 percent per year that would otherwise be incurred but for the prohibition in paragraph ( 1 ) is forgiven.

( 3 ) Prevention of acceleration of principal. The amount of any periodic payment due from a servicemember under the terms of the instrument that created an obligation or liability covered by this section shall be reduced by the amount of the interest forgiven under paragraph ( 2 ) that is allocable to the period for which such payment is made.

( b ) Implementation of limitation.

( 1 ) Written notice to creditor. In order for an obligation or liability of a servicemember to be subject to the interest rate limitation in subsection ( a ), the servicemember shall provide to the creditor written notice and a copy of the military orders calling the servicemember to military service and any orders further extending military service, not later than 180 days after the date of the servicemember 's termination or release from military service.

( 2 ) Limitation effective as of date of order to active duty. Upon receipt of written notice and a copy of orders calling a servicemember to military service, the creditor shall treat the debt in accordance with subsection ( a ), effective as of the date on which the servicemember is called to military service.

I am a XXXX  consumer and hold two credit accounts issued by Synchrony Bank : 1. CareCredit ( medical financing ) 2. Living Spaces ( retail financing ) Both accounts involved deferred interest promotional offers that were represented as no interest or same as cash financing. The promotions did not clearly disclose that interest was accruing from the date of purchase at an APR exceeding 29 %, and that this interest would be retroactively applied if the balance was not paid in full by the promotion end date. 

Once the promotional period expired, both accounts were charged extremely high APRs in some cases exceeding 32 % far above the maximum rates permitted under XXXX Finance Code Chapters 302 and 303 for consumer loans.

When I contacted Synchrony Bank to request hardship assistance due to financial difficulty, they refused to offer any meaningful repayment options, refused to reduce interest, and merely referred me to a third-party credit counseling phone number. 

This conduct is : Usurious under XXXX Finance Code XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, which generally cap consumer loan rates at 18 % and allow recovery of triple damages for overcharges ; Deceptive under the Texas Deceptive Trade Practices Act ( Tex. Bus. & Com. Code 17.46 ) ; Unfair and abusive under the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) and CFPBs UDAAP standards, given that the retroactive interest terms were inadequately disclosed ; and Part of a known pattern of misconduct for which Synchrony/CareCredit has previously been subject to enforcement actions by the CFPB and state Attorneys General.

I request the following relief : 1. Removal of all retroactive interest charges assessed on both accounts ; 2. Reduction of APRs to XXXX legal maximum rate for consumer loans ; 3. Implementation of a reasonable hardship repayment plan ; and 4. Any further remedies available under state or federal law, including reimbursement of unlawful interest charges.

Date Account Event Amount Promo Terms Interest Outcome Notes XX/XX/XXXX ( approx ) CareCredit Account opened for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$6000.00} 0 % interest promotional period Deferred interest accrual from purchase date at 26.99 % APR Paperless statements ; Synchrony charges {$1.00} for paper [ Promo End est mid/late XXXX ] CareCredit Promo expired with ~50 % balance paid Retroactive interest applied ; new APR 32.99 % on new purchases Paid {$2400.00} in interest in XXXX and {$470.00} so far in XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Living Spaces Account opened {$1700.00} No interest until XX/XX/XXXX Deferred interest accrued from purchase date XX/XX/XXXX Living Spaces Promo expired Retroactive interest of {$1800.00} added Interest exceeded original purchase price XX/XX/XXXX Living Spaces Second promo purchase {$3300.00} No interest until XX/XX/XXXX Deferred interest accrued from purchase date XX/XX/XXXX Living Spaces Promo expired Retroactive interest of {$1900.00} added Again, interest close to/exceeding original purchase XX/XX/XXXX @ XXXX XXXX  Both Accounts Hardship request call ( 39 min, 3 reps ) Synchrony refused to lower interest or modify terms Offered only : ( XXXX ) close card & slightly reduce minimum, or ( XXXX ) credit counseling phone number ; condolences on fathers death but no financial help Key Points This Timeline Proves APR Spikes : APR jumped as high as 32.99 % above XXXX  consumer loan caps ( 18 % ) potential usury violation.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing a complaint against XXXX XXXX XXXX regarding misleading and deceptive financing practices related to their advertised interest-free or no interest financing. 

At the time of purchase, XXXX XXXX XXXX advertised and represented the financing as interest-free. However, I was not clearly informed verbally or in a way that was conspicuous that this was actually a deferred-interest loan, meaning that if the balance was not paid in full by the promotional deadline, interest would be retroactively added to the entire purchase amount. 

Only later did I discover that back interest was charged, which significantly increased the total cost. This material term was either not disclosed clearly or was buried in fine print, while sales staff emphasized interest-free financing in a misleading manner. 

I believe this practice is deceptive and unfair, as a reasonable consumer would understand interest-free to mean that interest would not accrue or be added retroactively. This financing structure was not adequately explained at the time of sale, preventing me from making an informed financial decision. 

I am requesting that this complaint be reviewed for deceptive business practices and misleading advertising, and that appropriate action be taken to prevent this conduct from continuing. I am also seeking a resolution regarding the interest charges applied to my account. 

The purchase happened in XXXX of XXXX.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing a formal complaint against Fifth Third Bank for inaccurate credit reporting, lack of required disclosures, and improper handling of a credit card account I never had access to.

Timeline and Facts : When my original Fifth Third credit card expired, I reported it as lost/stolen for security.

Fifth Third issued a replacement card but, without notifying me, created a new account instead of continuing my existing one.

I was never told that a separate account had been opened, never received new account-opening disclosures as required by Regulation Z 1026.6, and I never activated or used the replacement card.

Despite this, Fifth Third later claimed that statements were viewable online. That is inaccurate : the new account never appeared in my online banking, and I had no knowledge or access to it.

The first automated email I ever received saying a statement was available was on XX/XX/year>, even though the bank claims this account had been active for years. Those emails were generic and were automatically routed to a folder in my email and not seen as I get many for other accounts with them. That message made no mention of a new account being opened.

I have maintained a perfect payment record with Fifth Third and can document that I have never missed a payment on any account. If I had known a separate account existed or had access to it, I would have paid any legitimate balance immediately.

Dispute Handling Failures : I contacted customer service and spoke with representatives XXXX ( ID XXXX ) and XXXX ( ID XXXX ). Both confirmed the replacement card was never activated. XXXX refused to allow me to file a dispute or speak to a higher-level manager, stating that it was not a bank error. Fifth Third has also failed to provide : Written disclosure that a new account was created Proof that I ever had access to view statements Complete billing statements or transaction history validating the debt A written explanation of its no error finding Regulatory Violations : Failure to provide account-opening disclosures ( Reg Z 1026.6 ) Failure to provide reasonable access to billing statements ( Reg Z 1026.5 ( b ) ( 2 ) / FCBA ) Refusal to accept or escalate a consumer dispute ( FCBA and FCRA 611 ) Credit Score Impact : Fifth Thirds inaccurate negative reporting caused my credit score to drop approximately XXXX points within a short period. This decline appeared immediately after the disputed delinquency was reported, despite my flawless history with all other accounts. The negative mark has caused measurable financial harm and loss of creditworthiness.

Requested Resolution : 1. Remove or correct the erroneous Fifth Third account and all related negative credit reporting.

2. Provide written confirmation that I was never notified of, nor had access to, the newly created account.

3. Review and correct Fifth Thirds internal dispute-handling failures and staff conduct that obstructed escalation.

Supporting Information / Exhibits : Fifth Third Case # : XXXX Representatives : XXXX ( XXXX ) and XXXX ( XXXX ) First statement available email : XX/XX/year> Proof of on-time payment history and XXXX-point credit-score drop screenshots Evidence the replacement card/account were never activated or accessed Inability to access, pay, or view the statements that informed me of any debt. I did not know of this new account until it hit my credit report.

Relief Sought : Immediate removal of the inaccurate delinquency from all credit bureaus Written acknowledgment that the new account was opened without proper notice or access Restoration of my prior credit standing Review of Fifth Thirds compliance procedures regarding account notifications and dispute handling

On XX/XX/year>, I was approached inside XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX by several individuals who appeared to be students collecting {$1.00} donations for a school soccer team. I agreed to donate {$1.00} and completed a {$1.00} payment using XXXX XXXX on my phone. 

Later, I discovered that the merchant had actually processed a {$1000.00} charge to a TV repair business in a different city. The amount I authorized ( {$1.00} ) was not the amount processed ( {$1000.00} ). This is a classic deceptive donation scam. 

I contacted the XXXX XXXX Police Department and filed an official fraud report, which the department accepted as a criminal scam case ( tracking number XXXX ). The police confirmed that this type of donation scam has been occurring in local malls and that I am a crime victim. 

I also contacted XXXX, the payment processor. XXXX confirmed that this patternasking for a small donation and charging a much larger amountis a known scam on their platform, and that the issuing bank must reverse the charge. 

However, my issuer ( Robinhood ) refused to reverse the charge, stating only that XXXX XXXX was used on your device, even though federal Regulation Z says that a transaction is unauthorized if the amount differs from what the consumer agreed to. I only agreed to {$1.00}. 

The issuer also refused to provide the XXXX XXXX Device Account Number, which is required to retrieve the XXXX receipt and confirm the fraudulent merchant. This prevented me from gathering full evidence and shows that the investigation was incomplete. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance because my issuer denied a clearly fraudulent overcharge despite police confirmation, processor confirmation, and federal consumer protection rules.

I applied for a Capital One Savor card on XX/XX/year> - I was preapproved and a promotional offer was advertised directly to me ( get {$200.00} back when you spend {$500.00} in your first 3 months, in addition to unlimited 3 % cash back on dining, entertainment, streaming, and groceries ). 

I formally applied on XX/XX/year>, was approved immediately, and was notified that the card shipped to me as of XX/XX/year>. I received a call from Capital One Fraud Protection on XX/XX/year> saying that they need to verify that the application for the card was actually mine. I answered all of their identity questions and on XX/XX/year> provided all the documentation that they requested for further identity verification ( driver 's license, proof of residence for current address, a written letter explaining the situation, my case number, etc ) through a private email link. My permanent address on my driver 's license is different than my current address, which I explained very clearly on the phone and in the written document that I attached to the private document submission link. I was told it was no problem if I provided proof of both addresses ( i.e. driver 's license and a current proof of residence for the new address, both of which I provided ) The next day, on XX/XX/year> they contacted me again and said that " their system did not accept my proof of residence document ''. Instead, they said that I needed to submit a bank statement with full account number, name, and full permanent address. I did so immediately after the call on XX/XX/year>. 

They told me that if I submitted the bank statement, which contained my full permanent address, that my Capital One Savor card would be finally authorized for use. I made it very clear that my bank statement only contained my permanent address and he said that it did not matter as long as the address on the bank statement was also associated with my Capital One Account. He confirmed that my permanent address, which is on the bank statement, is in fact already on my account, and thus, after the bank statement is processed, the card would be unrestricted and ready for use. 

It is now XX/XX/year>, a full month after the card was approved, yet my account is still restricted and Capital One has not processed my bank statement. 

They are trying to restrict the account unnecessarily to eat away at the promotion timeline back so that they don't have to pay me the promotional bonus. I have already lost XXXX month of the XXXX month promotional period to get the {$200.00} at no fault of my own. 

They advertised this offer directly to me, preapproved me for it, formally approved me instantly, and then made it impossible to actually engage in the promotion. They created unnecessary and unreasonable administrative hurdles to prevent me from accessing the benefits that they advertised specifically to me.
root_cause_category: Account Opening and Identity Verification Process

The Genesis of the Dispute : An Unresolved Charge Ignites a Pattern of Failure ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) The initial incident that sparked this protracted dispute serves as a microcosm of the systemic failures that would plague XXXX XXXX for years. Evidence demonstrates that the Bank 's systems were incapable of correctly processing disputes, leading to a cycle of errors that harmed XXXX XXXX repeatedly over four years. 

This systemic failure is evidenced by the sheer volume of issues ; XXXX XXXX was forced to file at least 67 transaction disputes between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. This documented pattern of mishandling disputes and failing to secure the account after being notified of theft constitutes a clear abrogation of the Bank 's statutory duties under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ).

4. Compounded Harm : Devastating Credit Damage and Discriminatory Barriers The Bank 's procedural failures were not victimless errors ; they inflicted profound, tangible, and lasting financial and personal harm upon XXXX XXXX. This harm was compounded by discriminatory practices that systematically blocked her access to resolution, creating an inescapable cycle of financial distress and frustration.

First, the Bank violated the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) by furnishing inaccurate and derogatory information to credit reporting agencies. On XX/XX/XXXX, the Bank reported XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX account as " Charged Off. '' This severely negative report was inherently false and misleading because it was based on a balance containing disputed amountsincluding the {$630.00} chargethat the Bank itself later admitted were the result of its own " processing error. '' Reporting this balance as " Charged Off '' constitutes a willful violation of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2, as the Bank furnished information to credit reporting agencies that it knew, or consciously disregarded the risk that it was, inaccurate, given its own admission of a foundational " processing error. '' Second, the Bank violated California 's Unruh Civil Rights Act through discriminatory practices. XXXX XXXX has a documented XXXX  XXXX, a fact of which the Bank was informed. Despite this, the Bank systematically insisted on voice authentication as a primary method for account security and dispute resolution. This rigid, one-size-fits-all protocol repeatedly denied her full and equal access to the services necessary to protect her account and address the Bank 's errors. These barriers were particularly egregious during a period when XXXX XXXX was medically incapacitated due to a tracheotomy, rendering voice communication impossible. The Banks failure to provide this reasonable accommodation was not a separate, isolated harm ; it was the primary barrier that prevented XXXX XXXX from addressing the cascade of other billing and security failures, thus compounding the financial and emotional damages. This failure constitutes a distinct and actionable violation of California 's Unruh Civil Rights Act, which guarantees all persons equal access to business services regardless of disability.

5. Evidence of Willful Bad Faith : Coercion and Breach of Promise ( XXXX ) The Bank 's pattern of conduct culminated in recent actions that demonstrate a shift from gross negligence to willful, bad-faith behavior. Forced into an impossible position and seeking to recover the larger sum, the Claimant complied with this condition. However, the Bank subsequently failed to honor its promise. This sequence of events is a textbook case of fraudulent inducement and a clear breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. This incident is not a misunderstanding ; it is unambiguous evidence of a willful, oppressive, and fraudulent pattern of conduct that justifies punitive measures.

Third, an award for Litigation Expenses of {$1800.00}, representing compensation for 90 hours of uncompensated time XXXX XXXX was forced to spend compiling evidence, documenting errors, and preparing for this arbitration as a direct result of the Bank 's misconduct.

Finally, the Claimant seeks Statutory, Injunctive, and Punitive Relief, including : An order compelling the Bank to direct all major credit reporting agencies to immediately delete the " Charged Off '' status and all related negative tradelines from the Claimant 's credit files.

Continuing FCBA Violation : The Bank is currently holding an Accepted Refund amount of {$3800.00} since XX/XX/XXXX. The failure to credit these acknowledged funds is a fresh, continuing violation of the FCBA.

B. Fraudulent Inducement and Coercive Practices Fraudulent Condition : In XX/XX/XXXX, the Bank conditioned the release of the {$3800.00} held refund via direct deposit on the Claimant being forced to close a separate {$500.00} XXXX XXXX computer dispute.

Breach of Promise : The Claimant complied by closing the {$500.00} dispute ; however, the Bank failed to honor its promise, and the {$3800.00} refund remains uncredited as of this filing. This action constitutes fraudulent inducement and a breach of the duty of good faith.

C. Discrimination and Inaccurate Credit Reporting ( Unruh Act/FCRA ) Disability Discrimination ( Unruh Act ) : The Claimant, who has a documented XXXX  XXXX, was repeatedly denied equal access to account security and dispute resolution services because the Bank systematically insisted on voice authentication.

Inaccurate Credit Reporting ( FCRA ) : The Bank reported the account as " Charged Off '' on XX/XX/XXXX. This negative report was inherently inaccurate because it was based on a balance that included disputed amounts, which the Bank subsequently admitted were subject to its own " processing error ''.

II. CAUSES OF ACTION No.

Cause of Action Legal Basis and Key Facts I.

VIOLATION OF THE FAIR CREDIT BILLING ACT ( FCBA )/ TILA Failure to investigate and correct billing errors ( the 67 disputes ). Illegal assessment of eight unauthorized {$53.00} installments. Illegal retention of the {$3800.00} held refund.

II.

VIOLATION OF THE FAIR CREDIT REPORTING ACT ( FCRA ) Negligent and willful non-compliance in furnishing inaccurate information by reporting the account as " Charged Off '' on XX/XX/XXXX, based on a balance that included amounts the Bank later admitted were erroneous.

III.

VIOLATION OF CALIFORNIA 'S UNRUH CIVIL RIGHTS ACT Denying a person with a permanent XXXX  XXXX full and equal access to security and account services by insisting on discriminatory voice authentication protocols.

IV.

FRAUDULENT INDUCEMENT & BREACH OF CONTRACT Coercing the Claimant to close the {$500.00} dispute under the false pretense and fraudulent promise of immediately releasing the {$3800.00} refund.

III. RELIEF SOUGHT Claimant demands an Award against Goldman Sachs Bank USA, including but not limited to : A. ACTUAL DAMAGES ( Restitution and Financial Loss ) Category Claimed Amount Legal Basis FCBA Restitution ( Held Funds ) {$3800.00} Immediate release of the Accepted Refund on Hold since XX/XX/XXXX. 
FCBA/Contract Overpayments {$780.00} Full restitution for the eight unauthorized {$53.00} installments ( XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and six in XXXX ) and related interest/fees.

Loss from Fraudulent Condition {$500.00} Loss from the {$500.00} XXXX dispute which Claimant was forced to close.

Fraud Losses ( Other ) {$4500.00} Losses from other documented disputes and charges.

B. GENERAL DAMAGES ( Emotional Distress and Pain/Suffering ) Claim Category Justification for Damages Requested Amount Unruh Civil Rights Act / FCRA Compensation for severe emotional distress, anxiety, and humiliation caused by disability discrimination, years of financial uncertainty, and the resulting credit damage.

{$50000.00} C. LITIGATION EXPENSES ( Compensation for Lost Time ) Claimant seeks compensation for the specific, uncompensated time ( 90 hours ) required to resolve and compile evidence for issues created by the Bank 's negligence and willful conduct, calculated at a rate of {$21.00} per hour.

Activity Log Example Estimated Hours Spent Rate Cost Claimed Dispute Filing and Follow-Up 30 hours {$21.00} {$630.00} Evidence Compilation and Review ( 1,323 pages ) 40 hours {$21.00} {$840.00} XXXX XXXX Preparation 20 hours {$21.00} {$420.00} TOTAL TIME LOSS CLAIMED 90 Hours {$21.00} {$1800.00} D. STATUTORY, INJUNCTIVE, AND PUNITIVE RELIEF Statutory Damages : For willful violations of the FCRA, FCBA ( twice the finance charges ), and a minimum of {$4000.00} per instance of discrimination under the Unruh Act.

Injunctive Relief : An Order compelling the Bank to immediately delete the " Charged Off '' status and all negative credit reporting.

Policy Change : An Order requiring the Bank to implement a permanent, secure, non-voice authentication and resolution protocol for customers with documented XXXX  XXXX. 
Punitive Damages : Punitive damages are warranted due to the Bank 's evidence of willful, oppressive, and fraudulent conduct, including the admitted " processing error '' and the fraudulent inducement scheme.

On XX/XX/XXXX, I charged {$5600.00} on my CareCredit account. On XX/XX/XXXX, I charged an additional {$2000.00}. At the time of purchase, I was told verbally this was a no interest promotion, and I recall it being described as 24 months. I understood this to mean 0 % APR for that period. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, as I prepared to pay off the balance, I was shocked to learn that CareCredit had back-charged over {$3100.00} in interest retroactively from day XXXX of the transactions ( {$2100.00} on the {$5600.00} charge and {$1000.00} on the {$2000.00} charge ). I later learned the promotion had actually been 18 months of deferred interest, but this was never clearly explained to me at XXXX XXXX of sale. I was not aware that all accrued interest would be added if the balance was not fully paid by that exact date. 

When I first reached out to CareCredit in XX/XX/XXXX to understand the charges, I was in shock and tried to get answers. The customer service agent did not explain my options to dispute or offer any meaningful resolution. Only after doing my own research and speaking with others did I learn that I had the right to dispute these charges, at which point I called back in XX/XX/XXXX to formally open a dispute. 

I also never received monthly statements or promotional disclosures during this period because my account was tied to the wrong email address. This was not corrected until XX/XX/XXXX, when I called to check my account. Without statements, I had no way of verifying the promotional period or being reminded of the deferred interest terms. 

This practice has caused me significant financial harm. I fully intended to pay my balance within what I believed was the promotional period and would have had no issue paying a reasonable amount of interest for a short delay. Instead, I was hit with over {$3100.00} in charges that I never anticipated and that were never clearly disclosed.
root_cause_category: Misleading promotional disclosures and failure to provide billing statements

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding Bank of Americas online banking system and their handling of scheduled credit card payments and autopay.

Despite multiple attempts to enroll in automatic payments for my credit card ( to pay the full statement balance each month ), the system has been unclear and unreliable : On several occasions, the online portal displayed conflicting payment dates for my statement balance in full. For example, one screen showed my payment scheduled for one date, while another section showed the same balance scheduled for a different date.

To avoid duplicate charges, I canceled what appeared to be a duplicate scheduled payment. Instead of leaving one active payment ( which any reasonable consumer would expect ), all payments were canceled without clear notice to me.

As a result, my credit card payment was not made, causing my car insurance payment to fail, interest to accrue, and unnecessary stress.

Additionally, I received no notice to the account being passed due. No email, no call, no text. I was made aware the account was past due due to the insurance payment declined. I then became aware the account was past due on my account homepage.

I also received an email from Bank of America confirming a {$790.00} payment scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX, yet my prior statement balance was not paid as expected. 
Bank of America has sent conflicting emails advising that I have a minimum payment scheduled and confirmed.

I have contacted Bank of America customer service multiple times about these issues. The representatives could not resolve the problem, refused to escalate me to a manager, and instead offered to log a complaint without any meaningful action. One agent even asked me for account information that is already stored in my profile, which is unreasonable and unhelpful. 

This experience raises serious concerns about compliance with the Credit CARD Acts requirements for transparency and fairness. A consumer with reasonable diligence should be able to clearly understand when and how their statement balance will be paid. Bank of Americas system is confusing, misleading, and sets customers up for missed payments and interest charges.
root_cause_category: Systemic technical failure and inadequate customer service resolution

Complaint Narrative : I am filing this complaint against Chime Financial , Inc., regarding their mishandling of a {$3300.00} dispute ( Dispute ID : XXXX and XXXX ) for a transaction with XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX on my Chime Credit Builder Visa card . I originally filed the dispute by phone on XX/XX/2025, for goods/services not as described. Since then, I have experienced ongoing financial harm, conflicting information, legal violations, and outright neglect by Chime. Worse, CFPB has failed to intervene despite two prior complaints, one marked responded with no correction of the violations, and another wrongly dismissed as a duplicate despite being based on new facts.

PRIOR CFPB COMPLAINT FAILURES : My first complaint ( XX/XX/2025 ) was marked company responded, despite Chimes clear Regulation Z violations and refusal to issue provisional credit. No enforcement or follow-up occurred. 
My second complaint ( XX/XX/2025 ) was rejected as a duplicate, even though it described new developments including the rebuttal, ongoing hardship, failure to resolve, and conflicting info from Chime. 
This complaint is not a duplicate. It covers XXXX XXXX violations and includes new emails, call recordings, and confirmation that Chime found a billing error and still refused to credit my account. 

TIMELINE OF EVENTS & MISCONDUCT : XX/XX/2025 I filed the dispute via phone. Chime sent an email promising resolution by XX/XX/2025. 
XX/XX/2025 Chime denied the dispute with no documentation or legal explanation. 
XX/XX/2025 I submitted a formal rebuttal with evidence. 
XX/XX/2025 Chime claimed a chargeback was filed. 
XX/XX/2025 I notified Chime of financial hardship and asked for provisional credit. I also received an email saying a chargeback was already in progress for my rebuttal yet no date or timeline was given. 
XX/XX/2025 Chime finally sent the denial documentation, which was my own rebuttal documents. Meaning they used evidence I provided after the denial to justify the original denial which is impossible and a violation of Regulation Z. 
XX/XX/2025 My husband, who filed a similar dispute with a different bank using the same evidence, was refunded in full. 
XX/XX/2025 A Chime dispute representative stated on a recorded call : Chime has determined a billing error occurred. Were just waiting to force them to return your money. Despite this, no credit was issued. 
Chime staff repeatedly told me that pre-arbitration had been filed, while others said a new chargeback had begun violating Visas rules that pre-arbitration cant begin until an investigation is complete and a determination is made. Chime also said chargebacks are just merchant communications, which contradicts Visa Core Rule 11.3, where chargebacks represent a formal request for reversal and are not informal messages. REGULATION Z VIOLATIONS ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ) : 1. 1026.13 ( c ) ( 1 ) Acknowledgment and Denial Requirements I received no explanation of the XX/XX/XXXX denial. 
The XX/XX/XXXX email only linked to documentation that I had submitted, not evidence from the merchant. No legal explanation was provided.

2. 1026.13 ( c ) ( 2 ) Timely Resolution Chime was required to resolve the dispute within 90 days or two billing cycles ( XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX ), whichever is shorter. That deadline passed on XX/XX/2025. 
I received no resolution or funds by that date, violating this rule. 

3. 1026.13 ( d ) ( 1 ) Provisional Credit & Collection Bar I notified Chime of financial hardship on XXXX XXXX XXXX.

Under Reg Z, a creditor must suspend collection on the disputed amount and is expected to issue provisional credit when hardship is reported and the investigation continues.

No provisional credit was issued.

I was told on the call that Chime determined a billing error yet 1026.13 ( e ) says once a billing error is confirmed, it must be corrected immediately, including issuing credit. Chime has not done so.

4. 1026.13 ( f ) Explanation of Resolution I never received any proper written explanation of the denial that includes the creditors reasoning or merchant response.

VISA RULE VIOLATIONS : Per Visa Core Rules Section 11.3.2 : The Issuer should provide provisional credit to the Cardholders account in the amount of the dispute during the investigation period.

Per Visas Interlink Operating Regulations : An Issuer must provide provisional credit for the amount of a dispute or unauthorized transaction within 5 business days of notification of hardship or investigation delay.

Chime has violated Visas rules by : Not issuing provisional credit during an extended investigation.

Misrepresenting the chargeback process.

Claiming pre-arbitration was filed before final resolution, violating Visa process rules.

DECEPTIVE, ABUSIVE, AND UNFAIR PRACTICES : I was repeatedly hung up on by Chime supervisors for quality reasons despite remaining calm and respectful. I have call recordings.

Chime refused to answer questions and contradicted itself constantly.

One rep claimed my rebuttal reset the timeline a false and misleading statement under Regulation Z. 
Chimes email on XX/XX/XXXX promised resolution by XX/XX/XXXX, but theyve ignored that. The XX/XX/XXXX email confirmed a chargeback was already in progress, yet gave no deadline. 
I am a longtime Chime user of 5 years with 3 disabled children whose SSI benefits are deposited into Chime. This mishandling has caused hardship, mental distress, and late fees for rent and utilities. My kids and I have had to skip meals due to this.

FINAL DEMAND : I am requesting the CFPB investigate and enforce the following : 1. Immediate credit of {$3300.00} to my Chime Credit Builder account.

2. Full explanation of the June 3 denial and all documentation used.

3. Confirmation of chargeback and pre-arbitration dates.

4. Explanation for the failure to issue provisional credit after XX/XX/XXXX hardship notice. 
5. Explanation of why credit was not issued when Chime confirmed a billing error on XX/XX/XXXX. 
6. Investigation into whether Chime is in systemic violation of Regulation Z and Visa rules.

7. Review of CFPBs own mishandling of prior complaints, including wrongful duplicate designation and lack of regulatory enforcement despite clear legal breaches.

I will add additional attachments upon request as all of my documentation uploaded prior has never been reviewed.

On XX/XX/year>, I received a new card for my Discover account in the mail, in which it was communicated my credit line was {$8000.00} ( I have email confirmation of the delivery ). After an expensive holiday season between my girlfriend and my families, I was nearing {$6000.00} used and started XXXX deciding to slow down spending in order to retain ~ {$2000.00} in case of emergency. 

On XX/XX/year>, I ended up needing to visit an emergency dentist for a broken molar. Due to the emergency nature of the visit, I was responsible for costs up front with the ability to submit a claim with my dental insurance for reimbursement afterwards. Upon costing out the procedures, I discovered that my credit line with Discover had been decreased to {$6500.00}, significantly reducing what I thought I had on hand for emergency. 

Due to this change in my financial situation came a change in my treatment. We were able to extract the tooth, but I was unable to afford an implant at that time. I instead had the gums sewn up where the tooth was. I will have to wait on my insurance claim in order to proceed with the implant. 

There is a high risk of XXXX that comes with this hole where my tooth should be. That can prolong my timeline to being able to get an implant. Should I wait too long, my mouth will heal in a way that will prevent me from being able to get an implant. In essence, this credit line reduction has put me in jeopardy of living with XXXX teeth. 

When I bridged this discussion with Discover, they told me that they had made and communicated the decision on XX/XX/year>. I received no mail or email communication of this. I only found notice with the help of my third representative in a XX/XX/year> phone call. 

I understand that my creditor reserves the right to adjust my credit line as they see fit, and that is not where my complaint lies. XXXX XXXX  ineffective communication of my credit line decrease, followed by a contradicting communication that my credit line was {$8000.00} led to a significant misconception of my financial situation. This impacted my dental treatments, landing me in a worse situation than I had anticipated.
root_cause_category: Communication failure regarding credit limit adjustment

These fraudulent accounts and inquiries are causing significant damage to my credit standing, impacting my ability to secure housing, credit, and financial stability. Subject : Complaining Regarding Junk Fees and Reporting Inaccuracies by Citibank and XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX Dear CFPB I am writing to formally file a complaint against Citibank regarding inflated charges and excessive fees to my account ending in XXXX. I believe these charges are in violation of several federal and state consumer protection laws including the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ), the Fair Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) and the Credit Card Act of 2009. This has caused significant emotional and financial damage. 
Details of the Complaint 1. Inflated /Junk fees : I have been charged fees that appear to be excessive and disproportionate. There are charges and fees of more than {$4200.00} as the balance on the account was {$3900.00}. The charges imposed by the bank were higher than the credit limit on the credit card. {$3900.00} was charged in interest and {$260.00} in fees totaling in excessive amount of {$4200.00} in junk fees. The amount that they have been reporting to the credit bureaus for the last couple of years has also been inconsistent ranging from {$3900.00}, {$7500.00} to {$8100.00}. These fees are Unconscionable under state unfair trade practices laws and appear to violate Federal Consumer protection laws prohibiting unfair, deceptive or abusive acts and practices ( UDAAP ). I also believe they might be higher than reasonable or typical industry standards.

2. Unclear or misleading Disclosures : The fees and charges associated with my account were not clearly disclosed from start in a manner that was easily understandable I also did not receive adequate or transparent information about the full terms and fees before I opened the account. This is a clear violation of Tila and Credit Card Act 3. Reporting Inaccuracies : Citibank and XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX have continued to report inaccuracies to credit bureaus which has negatively impacted my credit score. Despite my attempts to resolve this issue directly with them. They have continually failed to correct or adjust the excessive fees and interest leading to account inaccuracies ; this is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act which requires accurate reporting of consumer credit information Impact : The excessive fees are not only inflated but appear to be punitive and have been added without justification. Additionally, this practice creates unfair and deceptive debt collection process causing financial harm. Inflating the debt from the principal of {$3900.00} to {$8100.00} by using junk fees has damaged my creditworthiness through inaccurate reporting. I believe this constitutes a violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) and the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) as well as other applicable consumer protection laws like usury laws, fair lending and anti-discrimination laws among others.

In XXXX of XXXX, I enrolled in a Barclays offer for the JetBlue Plus Card. The offer said " when you use your JetBlue Plus Card you're earning 2 tiles for every {$1000.00} spent on everyday purchases, and/or 2 tiles for every {$100.00} spent on eligible JetBlue purchases XXXX XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, up to 6 additional tiles. '' JetBlue tiles are used by JetBlue to track levels of status. Earning 100 JetBlue tiles gets Mosaic 2 status. Not including this offer in XXXX, I had earned XXXX JetBlue Tiles. With this offer, I should have earned an additional 6 tiles and got Mosaic 2 status since I spent {$550.00} on JetBlue purchases on the card and {$1300.00} in total spend on the card from XX/XX/XXXX till XX/XX/XXXX. Unfortunately, Barclays only sent over 1 bonus tile to JetBlue, and I had XXXX total JetBlue tiles for XXXX and not XXXX total. This was despite clearly meeting the spend requirements to receive the maximum of 6 bonus tiles. 

I called Barclays to open a claim on XX/XX/XXXX to get the additional 5 bonus tiles I was entitled to. The claim was closed without a response a couple weeks later. I called Barclays again XX/XX/XXXX to get it resolved. The agent agreed that that based on my spend, I should have gotten the maximum of 6 bonus tiles- she said she would send a request to the rewards team to send over 6 tiles to JetBlue instead of the 1 they sent me. 

Unfortunately, I got a phone call from a JetBlue Plus Card manager at Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX telling me that the case had been closed by the rewards team on XX/XX/XXXX without giving me the 6 bonus tiles. The manager said that I was not entitled to the bonus tiles for the {$550.00} in JetBlue flight purchases on the card because Barclays had discretion to not issue bonus tiles for every {$100.00} in JetBlue spend. 

I believe this is deliberately deceptive because the offer clearly says " when you use your JetBlue Plus Card you're earning 2 tiles for every {$1000.00} spent on everyday purchases, and/or 2 tiles for every {$100.00} spent on eligible JetBlue purchases XXXX XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, up to 6 additional tiles. ". Barclays could have chosen to only give bonus tiles for everyday purchases in their offer, but they clearly specified that they were also giving bonus tiles for JetBlue purchases. If Barclays had no intention of ever giving bonus tiles on the JetBlue purchases on this offer, they should have never mentioned the bonus tiles for JetBlue purchases.

I have attached a screenshot of the offer, screenshot showing that Barclays only sent 1 bonus tile to my JetBlue account for the offer, and screenshots of my Barclays JetBlue Plus Card bank purchases showing that I made {$550.00} in JetBlue purchases on the card during the offer period and {$1300.00} in total purchases on the card.

Complaint Against : WELLS FARGO Account Number ( if applicable ) : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX & XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Summary of Complaint : I am filing this complaint against WELLS FARGO due to its deceptive financial practices, including unauthorized securitization of my financial instruments, failure to provide full disclosure regarding fractional reserve banking practices, and refusal to acknowledge my legal requests for a complete accounting of pledged collateral usage. 

I have previously sent WELLS FARGO and its indentured trustee notarized Power of Attorney ( POA ) documents and formal requests regarding its participation in the Borrower-in-Custody ( BIC ) program. I have also provided evidence that my financial instruments were utilized in fractional reserve banking transactions. Despite these notices, the company has failed to respond adequately, continued collection attempts, and has caused financial harm. 

WELLS FARGO is an active participant in The Federal Reserve 's Borrower-in-Custody ( BIC ) program, which permits depository institutions to hold loan collateral while leveraging these assets for liquidity and additional lending capacity. 

Under the BIC Program, financial institutions, including WELLS FARGO, receive funds and liquidity support from The Federal Reserve in exchange for pledged collateral, which includes promissory notes and similar instruments deposited by borrowers. 

Borrower provided a ENDORSED CREDIT AGREEMENT and DEBT INSTRUMENTS for credit obligations. These instruments, recognized as legal tender and eligible paper under The Federal Reserve Act and Title IV, 401, should have been accepted at par value. 

Wells Fargo refused to honor these instruments, despite their classification as valid tender per 59 Stat. 237 2, thus violating federal law and engaging in deceptive financial practices. 

Wells Fargo has engaged in fractional reserve banking, wherein it accepts deposits and uses them to issue loans in excess of the actual deposit amount, profiting from the expansion of the money supply without properly crediting or offsetting the borrower 's account. 

Under the Uniform Commercial Code ( UCC ) 3-104, 9-203, and 9-207, the creditor has a fiduciary obligation to provide full disclosure regarding the handling of pledged collateral and to account for funds derived from its monetization. 

The creditor has unjustly enriched itself by leveraging borrowerss financial instruments for liquidity and additional lending while still attempting to collect payment, a clear violation of common law offset principles and constitutional protections under the Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments.

Details of Misconduct : Failure to Provide Accounting : I requested a full accounting of all fractional reserve transactions and pledged collateral usage related to my financial instruments. WELLS FARGO has refused to comply, violating transparency and disclosure obligations. 

Unauthorized Securitization & Unjust Enrichment : My financial instruments were securitized without my consent, allowing the company to generate profits while still attempting to collect payments from me. This constitutes unjust enrichment. 

Fraudulent Collection Practices : Despite being aware of its involvement in the BIC program and my lawful objections, WELLS FARGO has continued collection efforts. This is deceptive and unlawful. 

Credit Reporting Violations : The company has placed negative marks on my credit report, even though it has been compensated through the Federal Reserves discount window.

WELLS FARGO has engaged in deceptive and unlawful financial practices that violate my rights as a borrower. I urge the CFPB to investigate these matters and take enforcement action to prevent further consumer harm. If the CFPB is not able to help with this matter i have attached the complaint i will be filing with The federal District Court.

Subject : Formal Complaint Regarding Account Locking, Unjustified Restrictions & Poor Customer Service I am writing to file a formal complaint against Capital One regarding misleading business practices, breach of contract, and unfair treatment of customers. Specifically, I am frustrated with the unjustified locking of my new Capital One credit card which has a balance of less than {$30.00} and a {$2000.00} credit limit. The card was arbitrarily locked in XXXX XXXX  due to what their automated system flagged as suspicious activity during a routine gas station purchase. Despite verifying my identity multiple times and requesting that the account be unlocked, I have been repeatedly told that it remains locked for my protection, with no clear or legitimate explanation. 
My question is : how is locking my credit card after Ive verified my identity in any way protecting me? 
There were no suspicious transactions on my account, nor were any charges made that I did not authorize. Capital Ones marketing materials promise easy, instant access to credit and a hassle-free experience. Unfortunately, my experience has been the exact opposite. From the moment I used the cardoffered to me through a promotional offerthe account was flagged by their algorithm for an entirely legitimate transaction at a gas station. As a result, my account was locked without prior notice or any valid reason. 
To make matters worse, when I contacted customer service, I was told that my account could not be unlocked unless I provided three ( 3 ) months of bank statements from another bank account. This request is not only unreasonable but also invasive. If this information was required, it should have been requested before my credit card was approved. Given that Capital One already performed a credit check at the time of approval, this additional request for personal banking details feels arbitrary and unnecessary. 
Despite multiple attempts to resolve the issue, Capital Ones customer service agents have been unhelpful, repeating scripted responses without offering meaningful assistance or clarity. I was informed that Capital One reserves the right to freeze or close accounts at its discretion, yet no specific reasons have been provided, nor have I received a clear timeline for resolution. This lack of transparency, delay and uncaring attitude towards customers who got approved for credit, then denied using it, and the lack in resolving the issue feels both deceptive and unfair. 
As a loyal customer, I am frustrated and disappointed by Capital Ones lack of accountability. The freezing of my account, combined with vague customer service responses, suggests the use of bait-and-switch tacticspromising easy access to credit and then preventing customers from using it when needed. Additionally, I am concerned that this wrongful action may negatively impact my credit history, despite having done nothing wrong. 
I am requesting that Capital One immediately unlock my account and restore my access to the credit I am entitled to. Furthermore, I seek assurances that my credit history will not be affected by this unjustified action. 
After reviewing similar complaints from other customers, on the computer I assume this may be part of a broader pattern of bad business practices at Capital One. This seems more than a technological glitch and reflects troubling behavior from the company that needs to be fully investigated. As such, I intend to file a formal complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, and other federal and state agencies to hold Capital One accountable and to protect other customers from experiencing similar issues. 
Key Issues : 1. Unjustified Account Freezes and Closures Account Locking Without Explanation : Capital One has locked accounts without disclosing the reasons, often due to internal algorithms flagging suspicious behavior without properly verifying or contacting customers. This prevents access to credit, even when the account holds a valid balance. 
These algorithms, designed to protect both customers and the company, are far from perfect. They can be overly cautious or even discriminatory. A minor mistake or an overly aggressive AI system can result in unnecessary account locks, as happened in my case. 
Closing Accounts Without Notice : Capital One has closed accounts without informing customers, which negatively impacts credit scores and makes it harder to access credit in the future. In my case, legitimate transactionssuch as a gas station purchasewere mistakenly flagged as fraudulent. This should not have been considered high-risk, and while the system aims to protect customers, it backfired in this situation. 
2. Deceptive Fine Print & Hidden Terms Complicated Terms : Capital Ones credit card contracts, terms, and conditions are filled with confusing wording, binding arbitration clauses, and hidden fees. This includes sudden APR hikes and unexpected fees that were not disclosed upfront. 
3. Inadequate Customer Service Lack of Transparency : Capital One provides vague and conflicting information regarding disputes, fees, and charges, leaving customers without clear answers. My experience involved being transferred between departments for hours at a time without any resolution. 
Unresponsive or Poor Customer Support : Customers face long wait times, difficulty reaching service representatives, and frequent transfers between departments without clear resolutions. This delay, coupled with unnecessary demands for personal information and proof of income, feels like an invasion of privacy. 
4. Failure to Honor Promises Unfulfilled Promotional Offers : Capital Ones refusal to honor promotional offers, such as introductory rates and rewards, appears to be an example of unfair business practices that harm customers. 
Credit card companies are required by law to treat customers fairly, yet Capital Ones actions suggest otherwise. It seems that the company is more focused on exploiting customers by engaging in practices that violate consumer rights and fail to offer resolutions. While these actions are often justified under the guise of fraud prevention, they become arbitrary when not clearly communicated. Left unresolved, these issues appear to be designed to damage customers ' credit. I see why people are turning away from Capital One, it has a bad reputation, and this involves poor customer treatment.
root_cause_category: Account Management and Fraud Prevention Procedures

I am submitting this complaint regarding JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., in connection with misinformation provided to me by a Chase representative and the resulting financial harm I experienced after reasonably relying on that misinformation. 

In late XX/XX/2025, I contacted Chase regarding a late payment reported for XX/XX/2025. During that call, the Chase representative explicitly stated that the late payment would be removed from my credit report. Based on the representatives assurance, I instructed my mortgage XXXX to proceed with a refinance application. I have now spent months unable to move forward, as the promised deletion never occurred. In mortgage transactions, timing is essential as interest rates fluctuate, application windows close, and delays cause measurable harm. This is precisely the type of consumer injury the CFPB was created to prevent. 

In their recent CFPB response, Chase acknowledged the following : - Their representative assured me that the late payment would automatically come off my credit report. 
XXXX Chase now classifies that assurance as misinformation and states the agent should not have made that statement. 
XXXX Chase acknowledges that this representation was incorrect. 

Despite this, Chase maintains that it does not perform courtesy deletion and has declined to correct the reporting. 

This matter is not solely about disputing the accuracy of a credit report entry. Rather, this complaint concerns : - Misrepresentation by a Chase representative, - Detrimental reliance on that misrepresentation, - Documented acknowledgment by Chase that misinformation occurred, and - Financial harm resulting from that misinformation.

Regulatory Basis for My Complaint 1. Deceptive Acts or Practices ( Dodd-Frank Act, 12 U.S.C. 5531 ) Under the Consumer Financial Protection Act, a representation is deceptive if : - It is likely to mislead a reasonable consumer, and - The misleading statement is materialmeaning it affects a consumers decisions or actions. 

Chases representative made a clear, affirmative misrepresentation about an outcome that directly impacts creditworthiness, which is inherently material. Chase has already acknowledged in writing that the assurance was inaccurate and should not have been provided. 

2. Unfair Acts or Practices ( 12 U.S.C. 5531 ( c ) ) An act or practice is unfair if : - It causes or is likely to cause substantial injury, - The injury is not reasonably avoidable by the consumer, and - The injury is not outweighed by benefits to consumers or competition. 

I could not have avoided this injury once I relied on the representatives statement. Mortgage timing is sensitive and financially consequential. The delay caused by Chases misinformation meets the standard for substantial injury. 

XXXX. Furnisher Responsibilities Under FCRA ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ) While I understand that furnishers must report accurate information, FCRA also requires : - Reasonable procedures to ensure accuracy, and - Correction of information resulting from a furnishers own errors or misleading representations.

This situation falls outside normal disputes : The information itself may be accurate, but the consequences I experienced stem directly from Chases admitted misrepresentationnot the underlying facts of the account. 

XXXX. Duty to Prevent Misrepresentation ( Regulation Z Commentary, 12 C.F.R. 1026.36 ) The CFPB has repeatedly clarified that creditors must avoid misleading statements that influence a consumers understanding of their credit standing or options. Chases incorrect assurance directly influenced my financial decision-making. 

This request does not seek to compel Chase to inaccurately report information. Instead, it seeks an appropriate response where a consumer was misled by a financial institutions representative and suffered tangible consequences as a result.

I was having difficulty making payments for my business Gold charge card with American Express towards the end of last year. In XX/XX/year> I received several emails and phone calls with payment plan options to help me make the payments and regain my card membership. This plan had 3 options to make equal installment payments in 3 months, 6 months or 12 months. I chose to make payments in 12 months of {$370.00} because it was the lowest amount that I could afford at the time. At this point American Express had decided to close the account because it had been past due by over 120 days which I was ok with, but said that after making payments there might be a way to regain the account. So I proceeded to enroll in the program and setup the payments which they autodraft every month on the XXXX starting in XX/XX/year> from my bank account. I have made every payment since then on time. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I received an email to view my statement and when I tried to login I was unable to access my online/mobile banking account. I called American Express and was told that my account was in collections with a 3rd Party and they even offered to give me the collections agency number. I told them that I was enrolled in a payment plan and that does not seem accurate. I was then given a number to call to speak to a different department that handles my account and when I called I was told that everything was fine and that I was infact enrolled in a plan and that my account was still with American Express and wont be sold off to another party and that I should just continue to make payments. There was no mention of my account going further into deliquency. 

On XX/XX/year> my account was reported as charged off to XXXX by American Express. When I called American Express they said my account had continued to remain delinquent because I was not paying enough to cover the minimum payment due. This is where the deception lies, why did they continue to autodraft {$370.00} knowing it was not enough to cover their " minimum payment '' to avoid getting reported as past due. Why even offer it as an option without clarifying that this will not be enough to stop them from reporting your account as deliquent. There was no mention that I should have been making " more payments '' which then defeats the whole purpose of enrolling in a payment payment. 

They also told me that I should have called them to clarify how much I should have been paying and not just rely on the payment plan. I was under the impression that the payment plan they offered me was to help payoff the card in full and that the account was still with them and will not be charged off of sold to collections. Why would they expect me to pay minimum payments due and still enroll in a payment plan that does not cover this minimum payment. The whole point of enrolling in a payment plan is because I was unable to make the minimum payment.
root_cause_category: Misleading payment plan terms and deceptive collection practices

I request that the CFPB investigate Bank of Americas solicitations and handling of my application.

Complaint Against Capital One Bank ( Credit Card Fraud Dispute Mishandling ) * * * * Account Type : * * Credit Card ( XXXX, ending in XXXX ) * * Amount in Dispute : * * {$940.00} * * Merchant : * * XXXX XXXX XXXX * * Transaction Date : * * XX/XX/year> * * Original Dispute Case # : * * XXXX -- - I am filing this complaint because Capital One mishandled my fraud claim and refused to reopen my case despite new evidence a * * police report * * that proves the transaction was fraudulent. 

I placed an online order for store pickup using my Capital One credit card, but an * * unauthorized person picked up the product * * without my approval or knowledge. I never received the merchandise and did not benefit from this transaction. 

Capital One originally denied my claim because someone picked up the order. That person was * * not me * * and has no connection to me. Their pickup shows * * fraudulent misuse * *, not valid delivery. 
During a * * recorded call * *, a Capital One account manager clearly told me that if I obtained * * new evidence such as a police report * *, they would * * reopen my case even after 60 days * *. I relied on that statement and filed a police report. However, when I later submitted it, another manager told me the case could not be reopened because it was past 60 days. 

This is * * procedurally unfair and misleading * *. Capital One can not tell a customer that a case will be reopened if they obtain evidence then later refuse to honor that promise. It caused me significant distress and loss of trust. 

I have called Capital One * * many times * *, spending over an hour on each call. Each representative gave me * * completely different and conflicting answers * *. Some said the case was still under review, others said it was closed, and others said it could be reopened with proof. Managers repeatedly put me on hold for long periods, transferred me multiple times ( sometimes up to five transfers in one call ), and then hung up without resolution. 

Capital One sent me * * three letters on XX/XX/year> * * through my online account confirming updates to the case. The next day, after I spoke to a representative, I was told the case was * * canceled * * without any written explanation. 

This conduct shows * * gross mismanagement, poor communication, and disregard for consumer rights * *. Under the * * Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) * * and * * Regulation Z * *, I am not liable for unauthorized transactions. The pickup by a stranger was clearly * * fraudulent * *, not a valid fulfillment. I am requesting that CFPB review this matter and compel Capital One to : 1. Reopen my case and reclassify it as * * fraud * *.

2. Refund the full transaction amount of * * {$940.00} * *.

3. Issue a formal written apology for the mishandling of my claim.

4. Review and correct their internal procedures to ensure customers are not misled by inconsistent or false information. 

* * Attached evidence : * * * Police Report : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX # XXXX * XXXX * Copies of Capital One messages and denial letters

My accounts was never late! I have had exceptional payment history with XXXX  XXXX and all payments were placed on Autopay. This late payment that is reporting is a result on a systematic error on their end processing my payment. This was clearly a billing error made my the company and is not a reflection of my payment experience which violates USC Code 1681eb. This error has caused severe hard to my reputation, my character, my mode of living and to my family! I demand the late payment reported to be removed/updated. They are not in compliance with the following law. 15 USC 1666b and its requirements set the standards and the guidance for a creditor to treat a payment as late, yet they are reporting a payment as late and they did not comply with the law! 15 USC 1666b ( a ) Time to make payments A creditor may not treat a payment on a credit card account under an open end consumer credit plan as late for any purpose, unless the creditor has adopted reasonable procedures designed to ensure that each periodic statement including the information required by section 1637 ( b ) of this title is mailed or delivered to the consumer not later than 21 days before the payment due date. I have requested proof that this information was mailed or delivered 21 days before the payment due date and this information was requested in good faith pursuant rule 1002 and It was never provided. This is unfair, illegal, fraudulent, and not equitable to me as a consumer!

And instead of getting help when I was already in a tough situation, I felt like a victim all over again when I tried to get justice for a crime committed against me. 
Backstory

He said he had all this money from business loans and from his business being successful. He asked me what my biggest bill was. I told him my credit card bills were my highest bill. That way he can save on his taxes and help me. He made it seem like he really wanted to help me.  The first help he offered was for me to pay my credit card with his business account in the amount of XXXX on XXXX. He provided me with what he said was his business account number and routing number. I asked him several times if this was his account because I dont want to get in trouble. He assured me this was his account. He even asked for my name and address to report on his business taxes. He said you cant get in trouble for me paying your bills.  He assured me the payment would be good. I called and made the payment on my credit card. Once the payment was made it was supposed to be non-reversable. He told me to check the balance on my card and it should be XXXX  available. After the payment was made, he advised me that I would have to pay him half of the amount he paid for the credit card bill. He said go out and buy yourself something nice and Ill pay that off for you too. I spent my part of the card on something too, thinking he was just going to pay it off like he said. He said the next time he would pay off my card and I wouldnt have to pay him. He had me find out my daily max and what was available on my cards.  He had me set a pin on my credit card also. He had me open a XXXX account to send XXXX  to XXXX XXXX on XXXX He told XXXX XXXX  was his sister and that he got her a salon. He told me to go to my bank and do a cash advance on my credit card for XXXX  He then had me send a XXXX  payment in the amount of XXXX on XXXX  to his supposed to be cousin named XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  While I was at XXXX  he had me take out XXXX from my credit card and XXXX  to XXXX  him that to XXXX XXXX XXXX. A few days later, he had me check my cards and pay off XXXX more of my other cards. He even paid off a card I had that was closed so that kind help put my mind at ease that he was really in my life to help me and my kids. After the payment was made, he had me send him XXXX and some more money on XXXXXXXX to pay him for XXXX other credit cards. I also purchased a gift card for XXXX on XXXX. The gift card was supposed to be payment for him paying off my credit cards. After he paid the cards off that was when things started to change between us. At that point I started to question why he never asked, he never mentioned me having to pay him to pay off my credit cards. His response was this was only for the first time, and I would soon have some money once I started working for his company. He kept requesting money from me. Till I was so broke I didnt even have money to buy me or my kids food. Consumer complaint narrative:
What happened : I was misled by a XXXX XXXX salesperson into opening a Synchrony Bank credit card under what they described as an interest-free financing promotion. The salesperson never mentioned that this was actually a deferred-interest program. I was told I would pay no interest if I made my payments on time during the promotional period. 

I made every monthly payment on time and never missed a payment. However, I later discovered that Synchrony Banks minimum monthly payment amount was designed so that the balance would not be fully paid off by the end of the promotional period. Because of that small remaining balance, Synchrony charged me over {$3000.00} in interest, applied retroactively to the entire original purchase amount. 

Why this is unfair or deceptive : XXXX XXXX  salesperson misrepresented the financing as interest-free, not deferred interest.

Synchrony Banks payment structure makes it extremely difficult for an ordinary consumer to pay the balance in full within the promotional period. 
The required payoff amount to avoid deferred interest was never clearly disclosed on my statements or through clear communication. 
The practice appears intentionally misleading and predatory, trapping consumers into unexpected and excessive finance charges. 

Consumer harm : I was charged over {$3000.00} in interest despite having made all payments on time and in good faith. I would not have accepted the financing had the salesperson or the materials clearly disclosed the true nature of the offer. 

Requested resolution : Refund or removal of the $ XXXX deferred interest charge. 
Investigation into Synchrony Banks and XXXX XXXX  joint promotional and disclosure practices.

Enforcement requiring clear, upfront disclosure that the promotion is deferred interest, not interest-free, and that minimum payments will not pay off the balance in time.

Parties involved : Creditor : Synchrony Bank Retailer XXXX XXXX XXXX Amount involved : Approximately {$3000.00} in deferred interest charges
root_cause_category: Misleading marketing and disclosure practices regarding deferred interest

Consumer complaint narrative:
I signed up for a Synchrony Verizon Visa card and opted for e-statements when setting up my payment account once I'd received the first bill. That went smoothly for XXXX and XXXX, as shown in my account. Then I inexplicably started getting paper statements. When I finally got around to fixing that, I realized that they were charging me {$1.00} per month " paper statement '' fees. 

I discovered online that this is quite common for Synchrony, which issues a number of company-branded credit cards -- switching people to expensive paper statements " for their convenience. '' A chat employee has refunded the fees and states that they sent me an electronic notification -- which I certainly didn't see, and doesn't show on my account -- advising me to set up the account... which I'd already set up. 

My issue is not with getting a refund. It's that countless people are being charged these fees and are not always able to spend the time to get things corrected. Nor should they have to. In my opinion this is simply a predatory practice and should be corrected.

I am requesting that Bank of America acknowledge the check was not cashed, correct their internal status, and refund or reissue the cashback. ] This is a follow-up to my original CFPB complaint ( Case : XXXX ) submitted over a month ago. 

After my initial filing, Bank of America contacted me and assigned a Resolution Specialist. However, after our first conversation, the specialist became unreachable. As a consumer, I feel I am being treated unfairly and that Bank of America is using procedural deflection to avoid returning money they never actually paid. 

I am now requesting that Bank of America immediately acknowledge the error, correct their internal status, and either reissue the {$4800.00} cashback or credit the amount back to my credit card rewards balance.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing this complaint against Navy Federal Credit Union ( NFCU ) for violating Regulation XXXX  ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) in connection with a credit card billing dispute involving BestBuy.com. NFCU denied my dispute despite clear evidence that the merchant provided contradictory and false information about the transaction. 

Timeline of Events XX/XX/year> : I placed an online order with XXXX XXXX for {$740.00}. 

XX/XX/year> : XXXX XXXX sent an email confirming the order was canceled and that a refund had been processed. 

Later communications from XXXX XXXX conflicted with this, falsely claiming that the merchandise was delivered instead of canceled. 

No delivery ever occurred, and there is no valid signature, photo, or receipt showing that I or anyone in my household accepted delivery. 

Despite these contradictions and my documentation proving the order was never received, Navy Federal sided with XXXX XXXX, stating in their final letter that the merchant provided documentation showing that a carrier successfully delivered the merchandise to the address requested. 

However, Navy Federal never provided me with a copy of this so-called documentation, nor did they conduct an independent investigation as required by Regulation Z ( XXXX CFR XXXX ( f ) ), which mandates a fair review of all consumer evidence. 

Violations and Concerns XXXX.Failure to Conduct a Reasonable Investigation : Navy Federal relied solely on Best Buys claims, without validating delivery proof, signature, or merchant contradictions. This violates the requirement under Regulation XXXX that a creditor must conduct a reasonable investigation before denying a billing error claim. 

XXXX.Failure to Provide Explanation and Documentation : Their final letter provided no detailed reasoning, no delivery confirmation, and no opportunity for rebuttal only a general statement that the merchant delivered the merchandise. This fails the disclosure and explanation requirement of XXXX ( f ) ( XXXX ). 

XXXX.Retaliatory Conduct and Pattern of Mishandling : I have previously filed legitimate disputes and complaints with NFCU, including a large debit card dispute with XXXX that was also mishandled. I believe XXXX XXXX may be engaging in retaliatory or dismissive behavior due to my past CFPB filings and dispute activity. Their repeated pattern of vague responses and denial without proper review strongly suggests bias or procedural negligence. 

XXXX.Delay and Procedural Misrepresentation : In earlier correspondence, Navy Federal stated it could take up to 4 months for resolution and that a temporary credit may not be issued. This is misleading and inconsistent with Regulation XXXX  timelines ( billing error resolution must be completed within 90 days of notification ).
